title: Suspicious Login Activity
description: A student account shows unexpected logins from unfamiliar locations. Reconstruct the browser timeline to identify when and how the sessions occurred.

events.explanation: The user began by searching for the official university login page, establishing intent to access the portal.

relationships.label: A search for the login page often precedes a direct visit to that page.
A login page search commonly appears before session cookies are created.

hints: Searches usually identify the pages the user intends to open next.
A search for a login page commonly appears before a direct visit to that login page.

events.description: Visited https://portal.westfield-uni.ac.uk/login (title: Student Portal – Sign In)
events.explanation: The search result led directly to the university authentication page.

relationships.label: Loading an authenticated login page often precedes session cookies being set.

hints: A visit to a login page usually comes before related session cookies appear.
Direct navigation to a portal login page is a common precursor to authentication artefacts.

events.description: Cookie set: PHPSESSID on portal.westfield-uni.ac.uk (expires session)
events.explanation: A session cookie was issued after the login page loaded, indicating an active browser session with the portal.

relationships.label: Session cookies are commonly set after a user loads an authenticated page.
Successful authentication usually leads to a dashboard or authenticated area.

hints: Session cookies are commonly set after a user loads an authenticated page.
An active session cookie often appears after visiting the page it is tied to.

events.explanation: Successful authentication redirected the browser to the student dashboard.

relationships.label: A later password reset search suggests a change in account state after dashboard access.

hints: Dashboard access indicates an authenticated session has already been established.
Successful login is usually followed by access to an authenticated area rather than another login page.

events.description: Google search: "westfield university password reset forgot"
events.explanation: Hours later, a password reset search suggests either account compromise or an unauthorised party attempting credential recovery.

relationships.label: A search for password reset typically precedes opening a reset page.

hints: A password reset search often reflects a changed account state or lost access.
Searches for credential recovery normally precede attempts to perform a reset.

events.explanation: The attacker navigated to the self-service password reset flow, a common step after gaining partial account access.

relationships.label: Visiting a reset page usually precedes evidence of a successful reset or re-authentication.

hints: Password reset pages are usually reached after identifying credential recovery options.
A reset flow visit often precedes account-level cookie or token changes.

events.explanation: A persistent authentication token was written, confirming the password reset or re-login completed successfully.

relationships.label: A persistent authentication token often appears after a reset page has been completed.

hints: A persistent authentication token indicates the account state was changed or restored.
New or updated auth cookies are strong indicators of a completed authentication change.
